• This critical test, performed at the Aeronautical Test Range in Chitradurga, Karnataka, involved releasing the RLV from a helicopter at an altitude, followed by an autonomous glide and precise landing on the runway.
• The RLV-TD (Technology Demonstrator) program aims to develop all the necessary technologies for a Two Stage To Orbit (TSTO) fully reusable launch vehicle.
• This technology is crucial for reducing the cost of space access and enabling future manned missions, including India's Gaganyaan program, by making space travel more sustainable.
• The successful test validates ISRO's capabilities in advanced aerospace engineering and propels India closer to developing next-generation space transportation systems.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of Space.
prelimsPoints: ISRO's RLV-TD program aims for Two Stage To Orbit (TSTO) capability.
RLV technology reduces cost of space launches.
Gaganyaan mission is India's first manned space mission.
Test location: Aeronautical Test Range, Chitradurga, Karnataka.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) technology for India's space program and its implications for future space exploration.
Analyze how RLV technology can contribute to making space access more affordable and sustainable.
Examine the role of ISRO in indigenizing advanced space technologies and its impact on India's global standing in space.
Evaluate the potential benefits of RLV for India's manned missions like Gaganyaan and commercial satellite launches.
GS3.imageDescription: Artist's impression of ISRO's Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) gliding for landing.
GS3.trivia: SpaceX's Falcon 9 is currently one of the most successful operational reusable launch vehicles globally.

GS3.headline: Government Approves Acquisition of Advanced Multi-Role Combat Aircraft for IAF
GS3.explainer:
• The Defence Acquisition Council (DAC), chaired by the Defence Minister, has granted Acceptance of Necessity (AoN) for the procurement of a squadron of advanced multi-role combat aircraft for the Indian Air Force (IAF).
• The acquisition aims to augment the IAF's strike capabilities and replace aging fleets, enhancing India's air defence posture in the face of evolving regional threats.
• The deal involves a mix of direct purchase and licensed production in India, emphasizing the 'Make in India' and 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' initiatives in defence manufacturing.
• This strategic move is expected to boost indigenous defence industrial capabilities and create employment opportunities within the aerospace sector.
• The new aircraft are expected to integrate state-of-the-art avionics, stealth features, and advanced weapon systems, providing a significant technological edge.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
prelimsPoints: DAC is the highest decision-making body for defence procurement.
AoN is the first step in defence acquisition.
'Make in India' in defence aims to reduce import dependence.
Examples of combat aircraft: Rafale, Tejas, Sukhoi Su-30MKI.
mainsPoints: Discuss the importance of modernizing the Indian Air Force (IAF) in the context of India's geopolitical challenges and evolving security landscape.
Analyze how defence acquisitions, particularly with 'Make in India' components, contribute to India's strategic autonomy and economic growth.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities in indigenous defence manufacturing and technology transfer.
Examine the role of the Defence Acquisition Council (DAC) in streamlining India's defence procurement process.
GS3.imageDescription: Advanced multi-role combat aircraft performing an aerial maneuver.
GS3.trivia: India is one of the largest importers of defence equipment globally, but efforts are underway to boost indigenous production.

GS3.headline: India Pledges Increased Contribution to Green Climate Fund at UN Climate Summit
GS3.explainer:
• At the sidelines of the ongoing UN Climate Summit, India announced a significant increase in its financial pledge to the Green Climate Fund (GCF), reaffirming its commitment to global climate action.
• The enhanced contribution aims to support developing countries in their efforts towards climate change mitigation and adaptation, particularly focusing on vulnerable nations.
• India emphasized the principle of Common but Differentiated Responsibilities and Respective Capabilities (CBDR-RC), urging developed nations to fulfill their historical climate finance commitments.
• The pledge underscores India's growing role as a responsible global actor in addressing environmental challenges and promoting climate justice.
• This move is expected to strengthen India's diplomatic leverage in international climate negotiations and foster South-South cooperation on climate issues.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Disaster and disaster management.
Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth.
keyTerms: Common but Differentiated Responsibilities and Respective Capabilities (CBDR-RC)
prelimsPoints: GCF is a fund established within the framework of the UNFCCC.
GCF aims to help developing countries finance climate projects.
CBDR-RC principle in climate negotiations.
UNFCCC (United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change) is the parent body.
mainsPoints: Discuss the concept of climate finance and the role of mechanisms like the Green Climate Fund in addressing climate change globally.
Analyze India's position on climate finance, particularly its advocacy for the principle of Common but Differentiated Responsibilities and Respective Capabilities (CBDR-RC).
Evaluate the significance of India's increased pledge to the GCF for its international image and influence in climate negotiations.
Examine the challenges in mobilizing and disbursing adequate climate finance for developing countries.
GS3.imageDescription: Logo of the Green Climate Fund with a globe and leaves.
GS3.trivia: The Green Climate Fund was established at COP 16 in Cancun, Mexico, in 2010.

GS3.headline: Bharatmala Pariyojana Phase III Approved for Strategic Economic Corridors
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet has given its nod for the third phase of the ambitious Bharatmala Pariyojana, focusing on the development of new economic corridors and inter-corridor connectivity.
• Phase III targets the construction of approximately 10,000 km of national highways, prioritizing strategic routes that enhance freight movement efficiency and reduce logistics costs.
• The project will leverage advanced construction technologies, including green highway initiatives and smart infrastructure solutions, to ensure sustainable development.
• It aims to connect key manufacturing hubs, ports, and economic centers, thereby boosting regional trade and industrial growth.
• The initiative is a cornerstone of India's infrastructure push, designed to improve road network efficiency, reduce travel times, and integrate remote areas into the national economy.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
prelimsPoints: Bharatmala Pariyojana is a centrally sponsored and funded road and highways project.
Aims to optimize freight movement and logistics efficiency.
Focus on economic corridors, inter-corridor and feeder routes, coastal & port connectivity roads.
Ministry: Ministry of Road Transport and Highways.
mainsPoints: Discuss the objectives and significance of infrastructure projects like Bharatmala Pariyojana in catalyzing India's economic growth.
Analyze how the development of economic corridors and improved logistics can impact various sectors of the Indian economy.
Evaluate the challenges involved in large-scale infrastructure projects, including land acquisition and environmental clearances.
Examine the role of public-private partnerships (PPPs) and advanced technologies in the successful implementation of such projects.
GS3.imageDescription: A modern multi-lane highway stretching through a landscape, symbolizing connectivity.
GS3.trivia: The Bharatmala Pariyojana is the second-largest highway construction project in India after the National Highway Development Project (NHDP).

GS3.headline: Indigenous 5G Technology Rollout Reaches Tier-2 Cities, Boosting Digital Inclusion
GS3.explainer:
• India's indigenously developed 5G technology stack has now been successfully deployed in several Tier-2 cities, marking a significant step towards nationwide digital inclusion.
• The rollout, led by Bharat Sanchar Nigam Limited (BSNL) in collaboration with Indian technology firms, showcases the 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' vision in telecommunications.
• This achievement demonstrates India's capability to design, develop, and deploy its own advanced telecom infrastructure, reducing dependence on foreign vendors.
• The expansion of 5G services is expected to revolutionize sectors like education, healthcare, agriculture, and manufacturing, particularly in non-metro areas.
• It promises ultra-high-speed internet, low latency, and massive connectivity, fostering innovation and economic growth in smaller urban centers.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: Indigenous 5G stack developed by C-DOT (Centre for Development of Telematics).
BSNL is a public sector undertaking for telecommunications.
Benefits of 5G: high speed, low latency, massive device connectivity.
Tier-2 cities are mid-sized cities like Jaipur, Lucknow, Ahmedabad etc.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of developing indigenous 5G technology for India's digital sovereignty and economic growth.
Analyze how the rollout of 5G in Tier-2 cities can contribute to bridging the digital divide and fostering inclusive development.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities for public sector enterprises like BSNL in leading the indigenous 5G deployment.
Examine the potential transformative impact of 5G technology on various socio-economic sectors in India.
GS3.imageDescription: A network tower emitting 5G signals, with a cityscape in the background.
GS3.trivia: India's 5G standards, developed by the Telecommunications Standards Development Society, India (TSDSI), are aligned with global 3GPP standards.

GS3.headline: New Large-Scale Solar Power Plant Inaugurated in Rajasthan, Boosts Renewable Energy Capacity
GS3.explainer:
• A new 500 MW large-scale solar power plant was inaugurated in Rajasthan, significantly adding to India's renewable energy capacity and clean energy goals.
• The plant utilizes advanced photovoltaic technology and is expected to power over 2 lakh households, contributing to reduced carbon emissions.
• Rajasthan continues to be a leading state in solar energy generation due to its abundant sunshine and vast barren lands, attracting major investments in the sector.
• This project aligns with India's ambitious target of achieving 500 GW of renewable energy capacity by 2030 and its commitment to climate change mitigation.
• The development underscores the transition towards a greener economy, creating jobs and promoting local economic development in the region.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
prelimsPoints: Rajasthan is a leading state in solar energy production.
India's renewable energy target: 500 GW by 2030.
Ministry: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE).
Solar energy is a key component of India's climate commitments.
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential of solar energy in India and the factors that make states like Rajasthan ideal for large-scale solar projects.
Analyze how increasing renewable energy capacity contributes to India's energy security and climate change mitigation goals.
Evaluate the economic and environmental benefits of transitioning from fossil fuels to clean energy sources.
Examine the challenges in scaling up renewable energy infrastructure, including land acquisition and grid integration.
GS3.imageDescription: Rows of solar panels stretching across a desert landscape under a clear sky.
GS3.trivia: The Bhadla Solar Park in Rajasthan is one of the world's largest solar power plants.
